title: Southampton Challenge
seoDescription: Take a fast Southampton-style reasoning quiz with logic, patterns, deduction, and university-themed challenges.
pageTitle: Only 6% Can Pass This Southampton Test
summary: A fast-paced reasoning challenge inspired by university entrance thinking.

homepage.title: Could You Pass This Southampton University Test?
homepage.summary: A fast-paced reasoning challenge inspired by university entrance thinking.

questions.prompt: Which item is normally used for studying?
questions.explanation: A notebook is directly used for studying and taking notes.

questions.prompt: Complete the sequence.
questions.explanation: The pattern adds 4 each time: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#7a0019">4   8   12   16   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which shape has exactly five sides?
questions.explanation: A pentagon has five sides.

questions.prompt: If BOOK becomes CPPL, then TEST becomes...
questions.explanation: Each letter moves forward by one.

questions.prompt: Which number is the largest?
questions.explanation: 63 is the largest number.

questions.prompt: Which option breaks the pattern?
questions.explanation: The third row contains a different middle symbol.

questions.prompt: All students revise. Mia is a student. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If all students revise and Mia is a student, Mia revises.

questions.prompt: Which word is alphabetical first?
questions.explanation: Campus comes first alphabetically.

questions.prompt: How many E letters are in DEGREE?
questions.explanation: DEGREE contains three E letters.

questions.prompt: Choose the next prime number.
questions.explanation: 17 is the next prime number.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#7a0019">3   5   7   11   13   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which answer contains exactly six letters?
questions.explanation: Lecture contains exactly seven letters.

questions.prompt: Which shape comes next?
questions.explanation: The pattern repeats triangle, circle, square.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="25" font-weight="950" fill="#7a0019">△   ○   □   △   ○   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: If 5 books cost 40 units, how much do 8 books cost?
questions.explanation: Each book costs 8 units. 8 × 8 = 64.

questions.prompt: Which word means the opposite of 'ancient'?
questions.explanation: Modern is the opposite of ancient.

questions.prompt: If SMART becomes TNBSU, then LOGIC becomes...
questions.explanation: Each letter shifts forward by one.

questions.prompt: Some lecturers are researchers. All researchers publish papers. Therefore...
questions.explanation: The lecturers who are researchers publish papers.

questions.prompt: The more you take away, the bigger I become. What am I?
questions.explanation: Removing material makes a hole larger.

questions.prompt: A student starts in fifth place and passes two people. What place are they now in?
questions.explanation: Passing two people from fifth puts the student in third.

questions.prompt: Complete the pattern.
questions.explanation: The differences increase by 2 each time.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#7a0019">2   6   12   20   30   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Final judgement: which conclusion is most reasonable?
choices: The evidence suggests the claim may be true
questions.explanation: Careful reasoning avoids overstating the evidence.
